summary: Verified the 3 user-reported fixes. Backend: all 4 pytest cases PASS (Issue1 P0 notification, Issue2 pending-alerts branch_id, Issue3 fee_zones persistence + zone-based customer fee + delivery-fee/suggest). Frontend P0: confirmed IncomingOrderCall modal appears for the cashier after a customer order is created — modal showed order number, customer name (TEST_فرونت زبون), items count, total IQD, delivery address, and Accept/Reject buttons; clicking Accept transitioned to the driver-assignment view (finish-without-driver visible).

minor.issue: Schema requires customer_phone (not 'phone') and delivery_location keys must be {lat,lng} (not latitude/longitude). 'price'/'product_name'/'total' fields are ignored — server computes from menu. Minor documentation gap — fields are not what the problem statement listed.

integration_issues.issue: Clicking the Radix TabsTrigger for value='payments' in the preview did not switch TabsContent — the active tab visibly reverted to 'المظهر' (Appearance) after click. Could not visually exercise distance-fee-toggle / fee-zones-section / add-fee-zone-btn / fee-zone-km-0 / fee-zone-fee-0 via the live UI. All data-testids exist in source at /app/frontend/src/pages/Settings.js lines 6692/6701/6707/6712/6726/6730, and the backend correctly persists fee_zones (verified by pytest).

action_items: Main agent: self-test the Settings → 'الدفع الإلكتروني' tab once — the tab content reverts to Appearance in the automated session. Verify the TabsTrigger for value='payments' actually switches TabsContent (it may be guarded by hasRole or a Suspense fallback that flashes the splash).
Optional polish: add a data-testid to the payments TabsTrigger (e.g. data-testid='tab-payments') to make the fee-zones UI walkthrough reliable in automation.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is now 23,917 lines — the customer-order handler, payment-settings handler and delivery-fee computation should be moved to dedicated routers/services.
IncomingOrderCall.jsx polls every 4s via GET /order-notifications — fine for one cashier but with many cashiers per branch this creates N polls. Consider a single WebSocket/SSE channel.

CustomerOrderCreate (server.py:20745) silently drops client-sent 'price'/'product_name'/'total' fields because the Pydantic model only has product_id/quantity/notes — acceptable but should be documented in the API spec.

success_rate.frontend: P0 IncomingOrderCall verified live; fee_zones UI source-verified only

seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_-prefixed customer orders to trigger order_notifications; corresponding new_order_cashier notifications were marked as read after each UI test to avoid polluting the next cashier session.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Customer order endpoint requires keys: items[].product_id+quantity, customer_phone (not 'phone'), delivery_location={lat,lng} (not latitude/longitude). The response wraps the order under data.order.id. Settings page in the live preview sometimes flashes a long Maestro EGP splash when switching tabs — wait >10s after clicking the payments tab.
rca of the issue: None — fixes work. Test data-shape mismatches were resolved by switching to the model's actual field names (customer_phone, delivery_location={lat,lng}) and using a real product_id fetched from /api/customer/menu/default.
